title: v3.4.2-rhel: Fix intermittently missing files in merged directory during llistxattr
body:
Addresses: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2008997 in the
v3.4.2-rhel branch.  Bumps c/storage to the version that was available
with this PR: https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/1037

Signed-off-by: tomsweeneyredhat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body:
@mheon @giuseppe PTAL.  This is my attempt to backport containers/storage#1037.  I did so by vendoring in the commit from that pr in c/storage (https://github.com/containers/storage/commit/7e016f3cfa9e869eb75c03e896dfe177ea80e69d), but the vendoring dragged in a whole lot more than I expected. Is this the right approach?

FWIW, we need to wrap this up by Monday in order to avoid the exception dance.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe*, *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v3.4.2-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat,giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@TomSweeneyRedHat  I think you have to backport this commit in c/storage first to the 1.36 branch if the intention is to only get the changes from that one PR.

Hmm, the latest branch we have in c/storage is release-1.33.  @rhatdan, WDYT about creating a release-1.37 branch from the v1.37.0 release, and then I'll backport that change there, then backport it all here?

Or is it better to go with this as is?

I got a LGTM from Giuseppe and happy green test buttons, but I'm leary about the extra baggage this dragged in.
I am fine with creating that branch.
Ugh, as I was merging into Podman, I realized I need c/storage v1.36 NOT v1.37.

Repushed using the right branch in c/storage, v1.36.  There is a lot less files here, but it did drag in some json related files that I was not expecting.  From c/storage, we're now down to just the expected changes in store.go to cover the issue.

Let's merge and we can bump it to an actual release after as Valentin suggested? @TomSweeneyRedHat @vrothberg please remove the hold if you agree

/lgtm
/hold

@vrothberg FYI, just spun a PR in c/storage to bump the version. Once done, I'll tag and then do another version dance here.

https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/1100
I'm in GIT heck in my sandbox.  I'm going to kill this PR and will create a new PR using the new v1.36.1 release
